success_surface: Multi-sheet column filtering with date-type detection and safe deletion ordering
effective_behaviors: Programmatic type-based column header detection using isinstance()
Multi-sheet consistent processing logic applied to all relevant worksheets
Reverse-order column deletion to prevent index shifting during batch operations
Post-save verification by reloading output to confirm changes across all sheets
Runtime date reference extraction for dynamic comparison thresholds
evidence: Iterated through ws1.max_column + 1 using cell(row=1, column=col) to detect datetime headers
Applied identical filtering logic to both 'Sheet1' and 'Result' sheets independently
Reloaded output workbook after save and verified row/column counts per sheet
Used datetime.now() to establish comparison threshold instead of hardcoded values

memory_items.title: Type-Based Header Inspection
memory_items.lesson: When filtering by column criteria, inspect header row cell values programmatically using type checks rather than relying on column positions or naming conventions.
memory_items.generalization: For tasks requiring selective column operations, use isinstance() or equivalent type detection on the header row to dynamically identify target columns.

memory_items.title: Batch Deletion Ordering
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple columns simultaneously, always sort deletion targets in descending order to preserve index integrity during sequential removals.
memory_items.generalization: Any operation removing multiple elements from a list-like structure (rows, columns, cells) must process from highest to lowest index to avoid offset errors.

memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Consistency
memory_items.lesson: When a transformation affects multiple worksheets, apply identical processing logic to each sheet rather than assuming uniform structure.
memory_items.generalization: Always iterate through relevant worksheets and validate sheet-specific metadata before applying bulk transformations.

memory_items.title: Post-Modification Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving modified workbooks, reload the output file to independently verify structural changes persisted correctly across all sheets.
memory_items.generalization: Final validation should read the saved file fresh rather than checking the in-memory object to ensure write operations completed successfully.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Type-Based Header Inspection
When filtering by column criteria, inspect header row cell values programmatically using type checks rather than relying on column positions or naming conventions. Use `isinstance()` or equivalent type detection on the header row to dynamically identify target columns.

### Batch Deletion Ordering
When deleting multiple columns simultaneously, always sort deletion targets in descending order to preserve index integrity during sequential removals. Any operation removing multiple elements from a list-like structure (rows, columns, cells) must process from highest to lowest index to avoid offset errors.

### Cross-Sheet Consistency
When a transformation affects multiple worksheets, apply identical processing logic to each sheet rather than assuming uniform structure. Always iterate through relevant worksheets and validate sheet-specific metadata before applying bulk transformations.

### Post-Modification Verification
After saving modified workbooks, reload the output file to independently verify structural changes persisted correctly across all sheets. Final validation should read the saved file fresh rather than checking the in-memory object to ensure write operations completed successfully.

### Runtime Threshold Extraction
For date/time comparisons, extract current time values programmatically at runtime rather than hardcoding reference values to ensure logic remains valid across execution dates.
